steps.title: Recognize co-interior angles
steps.content: When a transversal $t$ crosses two parallel lines $l$ and $k$, angles $x$ and $y$ are co-interior angles (also called consecutive interior or same-side interior angles). These angles are both in the interior region between the parallel lines and on the same side of the transversal.

steps.title: Apply the supplementary angle property
steps.content: A key theorem states that co-interior angles formed by a transversal and parallel lines are supplementary—they always sum to $180°$.

steps.title: Use the given constraint to find y
steps.content:
We're told $x > 100°$. Since $y = 180° - x$:
$$y < 180° - 100° = 80°$$
Therefore, $y < 80°$. That matches choice A — A is correct.
